key_metadata: Gut microbiome dynamics in IBD; 118 individuals reported as 49 CD, 60 UC, 9 healthy controls; longitudinal sampling; public study text reports 555 samples while archive run tables expose more run/sample accessions; Halfvarson et al. Nat Microbiol 2017, PMID 28191884, DOI 10.1038/nmicrobiol.2017.4.
uncertainty_or_blocker: Sample-count mismatch across study text and archive run rows. Use source-reported 118 subjects and 555 longitudinal samples, and note archive run-count mismatch in verification notes.

key_metadata: Blood-derived DNA methylation signatures of Crohn disease and severity of intestinal inflammation; GEO reports 402 peripheral blood EPIC methylation samples; Somineni et al. Gastroenterology 2019, PMID 30779925, DOI 10.1053/j.gastro.2019.01.270.
uncertainty_or_blocker: Same broad RISK family as existing RISK transcriptomic rows. Public metadata do not prove subject-level independence or overlap, so countability must remain Unclear until a RISK crosswalk is resolved.

key_metadata: Metabolomics Workbench serum and feces studies for integrative analysis of metabolome and microbiome preceding Crohn disease flare; serum study has 202 samples and feces study has 294 samples; publication reports paired omics from 80 CD patients and 43 controls plus microbiome samples; Levhar et al. UEG Journal 2025, PMID 40650893, DOI 10.1002/ueg2.70073.
uncertainty_or_blocker: Treat serum and feces accessions as one primary study-family row, not two independent cohorts. Publication linkage comes from title/abstract match and PubMed rather than repository-embedded citation.
